visual_notes: Screenshots taken from https://osortega.github.io/bloberto-office/. Desktop (1440x900): The office view is genuinely gorgeous — vibe-reactive floor, full character SVGs at desks (Sofia, Carlos, Maya, Dave, Luna all present), Bloberto at his CVO desk, golden-hour window, Ficus plant bottom-right, 'FULL SYNC' banner glowing, diagonal 'Clear skies — team at full strength' watermark on the floor. Sofia has a live speech bubble ('bugs have nowhere to hide') — reactive error bubble working as intended. Shortcut toast visible bottom-right. Mobile (375x812): Structurally intact — all workers visible, tabs readable, header compact. Office floor is cramped but functional. Worker names below desks are illegible pinhead text at mobile scale.

prior_issues_status.title: Bloberto hardcoded to never receive ping reactions — and now not even clickable
prior_issues_status.evidence: Office.jsx line 1551: <Character worker={bloberto} ... pingReaction={null} /> — no onClick prop, no PING_REACTIONS entry for bloberto. The blob does nothing when clicked. Third consecutive review.

prior_issues_status.title: activityLog.filter() called 4× inline per render — still not memoized
prior_issues_status.evidence: activityByWorker is now memoized (improvement!), but the Dashboard section still runs 2 chained .filter().filter() calls inline — once for the count badge (line ~1346) and once for the ActivityLog props (line ~1403). Still not wrapped in useMemo. Went from 4 to 2, but the fix needed was a single memoized var for both.

prior_issues_status.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES temporal dead zone risk — Office.jsx forward reference
prior_issues_status.evidence: Character component defined at Office.jsx line 545. WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES const defined at line 1146 — still 600+ lines below the component that references it. Code organization issue persists.

prior_issues_status.title: Footer quote cycling silently dead-ends — modulo added for display, not for cycling
prior_issues_status.evidence: App.jsx line 1425: setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p). Caps at 8. At quoteBonus=8 the counter badge disappears (condition: quoteBonus < 8 is false) AND clicking is silently ignored. The modulo was added to the DISPLAY (quoteBonus - 1) % BONUS_TAGLINES.length but the INCREMENT guard was never fixed. Classic partial fix.

verified_fixes: activityByWorker useMemo — VERIFIED ✅ (App.jsx line 942: useMemo over activityLog and activeWorkers. Well structured, correctly includes fadingMap workers.)
Touch device hover cards (pinnedCard) — VERIFIED ✅ from prior review, still working in code
Activity filter bar flex-wrap on mobile — VERIFIED ✅ from prior review, still present
Vibe sparkline with legend pills — NEW FEATURE ✅ (VibeSparkline component with last-3 vibe pills)
Ficus plant growth system — NEW FEATURE ✅ (5 stages seeded by vibeStreak, with milestone toast)
ConferenceTable standup detection — NEW FEATURE ✅ (standupActive when 3+ working + vibe is crushing or in-flow)
Ghost desk 'away' sign — NEW FEATURE ✅ (AwaySign component with tier-based data-tier and dynamic message rotation)
hashchange listener for browser back/forward — NEW FEATURE ✅ (App.jsx line 712)

suggestions.title: Bloberto still cannot be clicked or pinged — strike 4
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1551: <Character worker={bloberto} left={46} top={4} variant='manager' managerVibe={vibe} vibeKey={vibe} isSyncing={isSyncing} pingReaction={null} /> — pingReaction is hardcoded null AND there is no onClick prop. The Character component's handleClick gating logic at line 728 also gates on onClick existing. So: clicking Bloberto does nothing. No speech bubble, no ping animation, nothing. PING_REACTIONS has entries for all 5 workers but zero for 'bloberto'. Users absolutely try to click the giant manager character. Fix: (1) add onClick={handleCharClick} to the Bloberto Character render, (2) add PING_REACTIONS.bloberto array (manager one-liners like 'syncing team velocity', 'have you checked the backlog', etc.), (3) remove the hardcoded pingReaction={null} and wire it through the same pingedId state as workers.

suggestions.title: Footer quote cycling dead-ends silently at 8/8 — increment guard never fixed
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1425: setQuoteBonus(p => p < BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? p + 1 : p). BONUS_TAGLINES has 8 items. When quoteBonus hits 8: the counter badge disappears (condition quoteBonus < BONUS_TAGLINES.length evaluates false), clicking the ✦ button does nothing (guard blocks increment), and there's zero feedback to the user. The fix last review added the % operator to the DISPLAY expression but never touched the INCREMENT logic. The two-line fix: change the onClick to setQuoteBonus(p => (p % BONUS_TAGLINES.length) + 1) after quoteBonus > 0, or just allow it to cycle: setQuoteBonus(p => p >= BONUS_TAGLINES.length ? 0 : p + 1) to loop back to the vibe tagline.

suggestions.title: Vibe streak localStorage writes unguarded — effect will crash in restricted environments
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1036-1042: localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-key'), localStorage.getItem('bloberto-vibe-streak'), localStorage.setItem('bloberto-vibe-key', ...), localStorage.setItem('bloberto-vibe-streak', ...) — these four calls are NOT in a try/catch. If localStorage throws (quota exceeded, iOS private browsing with full storage, certain enterprise browser policies), the entire useEffect at line 1035 crashes, silently killing: vibe streak logic, streak eulogy toast, confetti burst on vibe rank increase, AND the vibe transition activity log entries. The vibe history block at line 1073 correctly has try/catch; the streak block at 1036-1042 does not. Wrap lines 1036-1042 in try/catch exactly like the history block.

suggestions.title: WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES defined 600 lines after the component that uses it
suggestions.description: Office.jsx: Character component at line 545 references WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES inside a useEffect at line 603. WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES const is defined at line 1146 — 600+ lines below. This is a forward reference that works at runtime (effects run post-module-load) but is a trap for extraction and tooling. The file has grown to 1660 lines. Any attempt to extract Character into its own file will produce a ReferenceError. Move ALL lookup tables used by Character (WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES, WORKER_IDLE_BUBBLES, DEFAULT_IDLE_BUBBLES, ERROR_BUBBLE_MESSAGES, AWAY_MESSAGES, WORKER_AWAY_MESSAGES, PING_REACTIONS, WORKER_WANDER_TOOLTIPS) to the top of the file or to src/utils/officeData.js.

suggestions.title: Activity filter bars use role='radiogroup' + role='radio' on buttons — incorrect ARIA pattern
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1362-1380 and 1381-1400: Two filter bars use role='radiogroup' containing buttons with role='radio'. The ARIA spec requires that radio elements inside a radiogroup be either <input type='radio'> or elements with role='radio' that implement the full radio keyboard pattern (roving tabindex, arrow key navigation). These buttons instead have individual tabIndex and rely on click. Screen readers will announce these as radio buttons that can't be interacted with using arrow keys (which is what screen reader users expect). Fix: either (1) use role='group' + aria-pressed buttons (simpler, correct for toggle buttons), or (2) implement full roving tabindex with arrow key navigation as ARIA radio pattern requires.

suggestions.title: activityLog still filtered twice inline — extract to filteredActivityLog useMemo
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines ~1344-1358: inline IIFE filter chain computing count for the section header. Lines ~1403-1412: second inline filter chain passed as `entries` prop to ActivityLog. Both filter chains have identical logic (activityFilter type + workerFilter name). With 50 log entries and renders triggered every 30s poll plus any state change, this is 100+ comparisons per render, duplicated. One useMemo: const filteredActivityLog = useMemo(() => activityLog.filter(e => { ... type logic ... }).filter(e => workerFilter ? e.worker === workerFilter : true), [activityLog, activityFilter, workerFilter]). Use it for both the count badge and the ActivityLog entries prop.

suggestions.title: window.matchMedia called during render on every polling tick
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 1212: window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches is called inline inside JSX during the render phase. matchMedia() creates a new MediaQueryList object on every call. Since polling fires every 30s and any state change triggers a render, this is a DOM query executing dozens of times per session for a value that almost never changes. Fix: capture once in a useRef or useState at component mount: const isTouch = useRef(window.matchMedia('(pointer: coarse)').matches). Or use a proper media query event listener if you want it reactive.

suggestions.title: meetingWorkers missing useMemo causes huddle useEffect to fire every render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1208: const meetingWorkers = idleWorkers.length >= 2 ? idleWorkers.slice(0, Math.min(idleWorkers.length, 3)) : []. This .slice() produces a new array reference on every render. Line 1246: useEffect(() => { ... }, [meetingWorkers]) — since meetingWorkers is a new array ref every render, this effect fires on every render, not just when the meeting composition changes. The lastHuddle ref update logic inside this effect is safe (it has guards), but it's wasted work and a correctness footgun. Wrap with useMemo: const meetingWorkers = useMemo(() => idleWorkers.length >= 2 ? idleWorkers.slice(0, 3) : [], [idleWorkers]).

suggestions.title: Worker name labels still illegible on mobile — 0.44rem is 7px
suggestions.description: Office.css at mobile breakpoints: .char__name is set to ~0.44-0.45rem (approximately 7px rendered). Confirmed in mobile screenshot — the names 'Sofia', 'Carlos', 'Maya', 'Dave', 'Luna' are invisible pinhead text under the character avatars. WCAG 2.1 SC 1.4.4 minimum is 9px for text. Since hover cards work on touch (pinnedCard toggle — nice fix), you can either: (a) bump to 0.6rem minimum on mobile, or (b) remove the name label entirely at mobile breakpoints and rely solely on hover card + aria-label on the Character div. Option b is actually cleaner.

suggestions.title: handleTabKeyDown missing useCallback — inconsistent with rest of App
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 778: const handleTabKeyDown = (e) => { ... } is a plain function declaration inside the component. Every other handler in App — handleTagClick, handleWorkerClick, syncFromGitHub, onResume, dismiss — uses useCallback. handleTabKeyDown is passed as onKeyDown to both tab buttons (lines 1249, 1262). Without memo on the tab buttons, this is just a style inconsistency today. But if anyone wraps the tab buttons in React.memo later, they'll get a stale props bug. Wrap in useCallback with [tab] dependency.

suggestions.title: completionToast and plantToast inline style objects — third callout
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 1158-1181 (completionToast) and Office.jsx lines 1307-1334 (plantToast): Both use massive multi-property inline style objects for fixed UI overlays. These style objects are recreated on every render. The only dynamic values are the border color (completionToast.color) and box-shadow color. Extract to CSS classes .completion-toast and .plant-toast, use CSS custom property for the dynamic color: style={{ '--toast-accent': completionToast.color }}. This is now the third review this has been raised. The file is also harder to read with 20-line style blobs inside JSX.

suggestions.title: No empty state for Dashboard activity log when a filter has no results
suggestions.description: ActivityLog component (App.jsx line ~155): when entries is an empty array after filtering, it shows 'No activity yet…'. But when the user has selected a worker filter or type filter that returns 0 results, the message 'No activity yet' is misleading — there IS activity, just not matching the filter. Should show 'No matching activity' or 'Try a different filter' when filtered.length === 0 but activityLog.length > 0. Small UX polish that prevents confusion.

suggestions.title: Vibe sparkline legend pills have no tooltip or accessible label
suggestions.description: VibeSparkline component (App.jsx line ~430): sparkline-legend__pill spans show vibe labels with background colors but have no title, aria-label, or description. Color-only communication fails WCAG SC 1.4.1 (use of color). The SVG circles inside the sparkline have <title> elements — good. The pills below do not. Add title={`${h.key} at ${new Date(h.ts).toLocaleTimeString()}`} to each pill.

suggestions.title: Idle micro-bubble emoji selection calls Date.now() inside JSX render
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line ~794: the idle micro-bubble emoji is selected with an expression that calls Date.now() inside the render return. This means the emoji selection is time-dependent on render timing rather than stable per idle interval. If the component re-renders between idle interval fires, the emoji can change. Extract the selected index to a useState updated by the idleBubble interval, not by render-time Date.now().

suggestions.title: Office floor 'Clear skies' diagonal watermark clashes with character labels on mobile
suggestions.description: The diagonal vibe weather label (e.g. 'Clear skies — team at full strength') is a large full-width diagonal text on the office floor. On desktop it's subtle and atmospheric. On mobile at 375px it spans the full office area and overlaps worker name labels and desk nameplates in the viewport. Consider reducing font-size at mobile breakpoints from current size, or switching to a small top-center badge on screens under 480px.

summary: Visual polish is at its peak — the office scene is genuinely delightful, the Ficus, the standup detection, the vibe sparkline, the golden-hour window are all excellent touches. The feature velocity is high and it shows. What's not showing is the maintenance backlog. Four HIGH issues are now on their third or fourth consecutive review unchanged: Bloberto can't be clicked, the footer quote cycles into a wall and stops, WORKER_ERROR_BUBBLES is still upside down in the file, and the localStorage writes in the streak effect are one private-browsing tab away from a silent crash. Accessibility improved — skip link is there, aria-labels are thorough — but the radiogroup pattern on the filter bars is incorrect per spec. Code quality ticked up (activityByWorker is finally memoized, nice), but the monolith files grew larger. The fun factor remains unchallengeable. Ship the Bloberto click fix. Please.
